text.title: API Monitoring — Alerting Decision Tree
text.summary: Routing tree from observable signal to a rule id in `01-core-rules.xml`. Every leaf resolves to either a rule id or `skip-this-methodology`.

decision-tree.root-question: Which observable signal best describes the current state of the artefact under review?

branch.question: Are alert rules being written before an SLO is documented?

conclusion.verdict: stop and write the SLO with PM/ops sign-off

branch.question: Does an alert lack runbook_url or `for:` clause?

conclusion.verdict: reject the rule in CI; require runbook_url + for: ≥2m

branch.question: Is the SLO protected by a single-window threshold alert?

conclusion.verdict: replace with multi-window burn-rate (1h+6h fast, 1d+3d slow)

branch.question: Is a `page` alert routing to issue tracker (or vice versa)?
Is a Grafana JSON being hand-edited?

conclusion.verdict: regenerate via jsonnet or grafanactl pull

branch.question: Is the env a POC with no production traffic?

conclusion.verdict: defer alerting until SLO + traffic exist
signal does not match; route to a different methodology or proceed without this one

section.title: Usage
section.p: Apply the tree top-to-bottom against the artefact (PR diff, config, design doc). The first matching branch is decisive; later branches are not evaluated. If multiple branches match, fix in the order listed — they typically depend on earlier fixes.
